memberdef.definition: using Dali::VectorBase::MemMoveFunctionType = void* (*)(void*, const void*, size_t)
memberdef.name: MemMoveFunctionType

briefdescription.para: The ratio of auto shrink to fit calling. @SINCE_2_3.22.
Pointer to the data.
Default constructor. Does not allocate space. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Destructor.

detaileddescription.para: Does not release the space. Derived class needs to call Release. Not virtual as this should not be called directly and we do not want a vtable for this class as it would unnecessarily increase size. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Helper to set the count.
Reserves space in the vector.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
count
Count of elements to reserve
elementSize
Size of a single element

briefdescription.para: Reserves space in the vector with custom memmove.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_2_5.10
count
Count of elements to reserve
elementSize
Size of a single element
memMoveFunction
Custom memory move function when we allocate new memory

briefdescription.para: Copy a vector.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
vector
Vector to copy from
elementSize
Size of a single element

briefdescription.para: Swaps the contents of two vectors.
Erases an element.

detaileddescription.para:
Does not change capacity. @SINCE_1_0.0
address
Address to erase from
elementSize
Size to erase
Last element cannot be erased as there is nothing to move.

briefdescription.para: Erases a range of elements.

detaileddescription.para:
Does not change capacity. @SINCE_1_0.0
first
Address to the first element to be erased
last
Address to the last element to be erased
elementSize
Size of one of the elements to be erased
Address pointing to the next element of the last one

briefdescription.para: Copies a number of bytes from source to destination.

detaileddescription.para: source and destination must not overlap.

@SINCE_1_0.0
destination
Pointer to the destination address
source
Pointer to the source address
numberOfBytes
The number of bytes to be copied

briefdescription.para: Replace the data as new data address. After replace, release the old data.

detaileddescription.para: It will be used when we want to keep the mData integrity.

Does not call destructors on objects held.
newData
new data address to be replaced

briefdescription.para: Fit the capacity of vector as item counts. It will be used when we want to remove unused memory.
This method is inlined as it's needed frequently for Vector::End() iterator.

detaileddescription.para:
@SINCE_1_0.0
The count of elements in this vector

briefdescription.para: Gets the count of elements in this vector. @SINCE_1_0.0.

simplesect.para: The count of elements in this vector

briefdescription.para: @ return if the vector is empty. @SINCE_1_0.0

simplesect.para: True if the count of elements is empty

briefdescription.para: Gets the capacity of this vector. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Releases the data.

detaileddescription.para: Does not call destructors on objects held. @SINCE_1_0.0

briefdescription.para: Deleted copy constructor. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Deleted copy assignment operator. @SINCE_1_0.0.
Deleted move constructor. @SINCE_1_9.25.
Deleted copy assignment operator. @SINCE_1_9.25.
Base class to handle the memory of simple vector.

detaileddescription.para: Memory layout is such that it has two size_t to hold the count and capacity of the vector. VectorBase::mData is adjusted so that it points to the beginning of the first real item so that iterating the items is quick. @SINCE_1_0.0
